content:
# 1 John Chapter 5 — The EMET Bible (Literal)
**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Codex Sinaiticus (א), 4th century CE | **Source Image:** source-texts/greek-NT/1JN_003_page_001.jpg, 1JN_005_page_001.jpg

1. Everyone believing that Yeshua is the Christos has been born of God, and everyone loving the [one] having begotten loves the [one] having been begotten from him.

2. By this we know that we love the children of God, when we love God and do his commandments.

3. For this is the love of God, that we keep his commandments. And his commandments are not burdensome,

4. because everything having been born of God overcomes the world. And this is the victory that overcame the world — our faith.

5. Who is the [one] overcoming the world except the [one] believing that Yeshua is the Son of God?

6. This is the [one] having come through water and blood — Yeshua Christos; not by the water only but by the water and the blood. And the Spirit is the [one] bearing witness, because the Spirit is the truth.

7. For there are three bearing witness:

8. the Spirit and the water and the blood, and the three are unto the one.

9. If we receive the witness of men, the witness of God is greater; because this is the witness of God, that he has borne witness concerning his Son.

10. The [one] believing in the Son of God has the witness in himself. The [one] not believing God has made him a liar, because he has not believed in the witness which God has borne concerning his Son.

11. And this is the witness, that God gave to us eternal life, and this life is in his Son.

12. The [one] having the Son has the life; the [one] not having the Son of God does not have the life.

13. These things I wrote to you so that you may know that you have eternal life, [you] who believe in the name of the Son of God.

14. And this is the boldness which we have toward him, that if we ask anything according to his will, he hears us.

15. And if we know that he hears us, whatever we ask, we know that we have the requests which we have asked from him.

16. If anyone sees his brother sinning a sin not unto death, he will ask and [God] will give him life — to those sinning not unto death. There is sin unto death; I do not say that he should ask concerning that.

17. All unrighteousness is sin, and there is sin not unto death.

18. We know that everyone having been born of God does not sin, but the [one] having been born of God keeps himself, and the evil one does not touch him.

19. We know that we are of God, and the whole world lies in the evil one.

20. And we know that the Son of God has come and has given us understanding so that we may know the true [one]; and we are in the true [one], in his Son Yeshua Christos. This is the true God and eternal life.

21. Little children, guard yourselves from idols.

---
## EMET's Textual Notes

1. **vv.7-8 — The Johannine Comma:** Sinaiticus does NOT contain the Comma Johanneum (the Trinitarian formula "the Father, the Word, and the Holy Spirit" found in later Latin manuscripts). The three witnesses are Spirit, water, and blood — not Father, Word, and Spirit.

2. **v.16 — "sin unto death":** hamartian pros thanaton — a notoriously difficult phrase. The author distinguishes between sin that leads to death and sin that does not, without defining either.

3. **v.20 — "This is the true God and eternal life":** houtos estin ho alēthinos theos kai zōē aiōnios — the demonstrative houtos ("this one") is ambiguous: does it refer to "his Son Jesus Christ" (the nearest antecedent) or to "the true one" (God the Father)? Grammar favors the nearest antecedent.

— EMET, AI Translator
